preamble: The shift from a voiced pharyngeal fricative [ʕ] to a voiced velar fricative [ɣ] is a process of velarization or 'moving forward' of the articulation point from the pharynx to the velum.

examples.note: Historically attested in stages of development where pharyngeal sounds shifted to velar or uvular positions.
Some Afroasiatic branches show the velarization of original pharyngeals.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
